2606.09134.title: From USD Scenes to Knowledge Graphs: Zero-Shot Ontology Grounding with LLMs
2606.09134.paper_summary: Constructing knowledge graphs from 3D simulation scenes is essential for robot task reasoning, but the key bottleneck, grounding scene objects to formal ontology classes, still relies on manually curated dictionaries that are brittle and do not generalize across assets. We investigate whether large language models (LLMs) can automate this grounding step for Universal Scene Description (USD) scenes as a zero-shot, training-free alternative. On a kitchen scene (125 objects) with SOMA-HOME Ontology, LLMs achieve 90-96% exact-match accuracy with descriptive names and 49-89% with abbreviated names, substantially outperforming dictionary and embedding baselines. Under fully opaque names, context-augmented prompting recovers up to 48%. Feature ablation reveals that LLMs primarily exploit semantic cues in the scene graph (sibling names and parent paths); anonymizing these cues reduces accuracy to 0-6%, while geometry alone yields only 4-17%.
2606.09134.paper_summary_zh: 從3D模擬場景構建知識圖譜對於機器人任務推理至關重要，但關鍵瓶頸——將場景物體基於正式本體類別進行基礎化——仍然依賴於手動編輯的詞典，這些詞典脆弱且無法在資產之間進行泛化。我們調查大型語言模型（LLMs）是否能夠自動化這一步驟，作為對通用場景描述（USD）場景的零樣本、無需訓練的替代方案。在一個包含125個物體的廚房場景中，使用SOMA-HOME本體，LLMs在描述性名稱上達到90-96%的精確匹配準確率，而在縮寫名稱上則達到49-89%，顯著超越了詞典和嵌入基準。在完全不透明的名稱下，增強上下文的提示恢復了高達48%的準確率。特徵消融顯示，LLMs主要利用場景圖中的語義線索（兄弟名稱和父路徑）；匿名化這些線索將準確率降低到0-6%，而僅依賴幾何信息則僅能達到4-17%。
2606.09134.author: Jiangtao Shuai et.al.
2606.09134.authors: Jiangtao Shuai, Zongxiong Chen, Manfred Hauswirth, Sonja Schimmler
